rulings.Case Title: JEREMY WIESEN VS. RAM TELECOM INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 10, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JEREMY WIESEN's Motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is unopposed and is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: ARTSIOM KHATKEVICH VS. B SIDE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Khatkevich'S Notice And Motion For Order Granting Leave To Amend Answer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 10, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ARTSIOM KHATKEVICH'S Motion For Order Granting Leave To Amend Answer. Cross-defendant Artsiom Khatkevich's motion for leave to amend answer is granted in substantial part. Mr. Khatsiom is granted leave to amend his answer by including all of his proposed new affirmative defenses except proposed affirmative defense 17 labeled "illegal contract." Proposed affirmative defense 17 appears to be based solely on opposing party's discovery responses that the contract is illegal as a result of Mr. Khatsiom's alleged failure to comply with the contractor licensing requirements. That is not a permissible basis for Mr. Khatsiom to allege an affirmative defense of illegal contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for B Side is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL P GUTA VS. CASEY SECURITIES LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Production Of Docs Responsive To Req For Production Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 10, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL GUTA's Motio To Compel Production Of Docs Responsive To Req For Production Set One Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: The court cites the following language from the Standard Language for Common Discovery issues dated January 15, 2016 and sent to all San Francisco Pro Tem judges. The rights of privacy of corporations is unsettled. However, even if there is a right of privacy: If there is a reasonable expectation of privacy and the invasion of privacy is serious, then the court must balance the privacy interest at stake against other competing or countervailing interests, which include the interest of the requesting party, fairness to the litigants in conducting the litigation, and the consequences of granting or restricting access to the information. Puerto v. Superior Court, 158 Cal. App. 4th 1242, 1251 (2008) Here, in general, the invasion of the rights of privacy are not serious as compared to the interests of the plaintiff in obtaining potentially relevant discovery. Request 1: This request is denied subject to Mr. Guta's showing that he does not have access to communications directed to him. Request 3: As drafted, the request is over broad. The request is denied without prejudice to Mr. Guta propounding a more carefully tailored request for these documents. If such request is propounded, Defendant retains all its rights to object. Requests 4 and 5: Granted. Defendant may redact the names of unrelated third parties. Request 8: Granted as to documents dated June 1. 2011 to February 1, 2016. Requests 10, 11, 12: Granted. Requests 13, 14, 15, 16: Granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: TITILAYO ALA VS. CH2M HILL COMPANIES LTD. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 10, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANTS CH2M HILL COMPANIES LTD., CH2M HILL, INC., CH2M HILL ENGINEERS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants CH2M Hill Companies, Ltd., CH2M Hill, Inc., and CH2M Hill Engineers, Inc.'s demurrer to the third, seventh and eleventh causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Titilayo Ala is: a) sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for gender violence; and b) overruled as to the seventh and eleventh causes of action for defamation and violation of the Unfair Competition Law. As an employer, per Civil Code 53.4(e), the CH2M entities are not liable for statutory gender violence because there is no allegation that they personally committed the violence. Federal courts are in accord with this understanding of the statute and Ms. Ala relies on inapposite authorities. The defamation claim is adequately alleged. Ms. Ala alleges that both defendant Richard Pyle and individuals on the Employee Relations Team made defamatory statements, yet the CH2M entities focus only on the defamation that the Employee Relations Team allegedly committed. Liberally construing the FAC, Ms. Ala sufficiently alleges that the CH2M entities are vicariously liable for Mr. Pyle's alleged defamatory conduct that occurred in the course and scope of his employment at CH2M. The FAC does not disclose on its face that the alleged defamatory statements are within the scope of the conditional privilege. Moreover, the FAC adequately alleges that the alleged defamatory statements were made maliciously based on the allegation that Mr. Pyle made up the statements. The FAC also alleges that the Employee Relations Team did not credit Mr. Pyle's statements but repeated them anyway. Ms. Ala sufficiently alleges economic injury to support her UCL claim. (Cortez v. Purolator Air Filtration Products Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 163, 177-178; Kwikset Corp. v. Sup. Crt. (2011) 51 Cal.4th 310, 323-325.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Ala is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TITILAYO ALA VS. CH2M HILL COMPANIES LTD. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 10, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT RICHARD PYLE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Richard Pyle's joinder in the demurrer of the CH2M entities to the third, seventh and eleventh causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Titilayo Ala is overruled as to all three causes of action. The first amended complaint adequately alleges that Mr. Pyle personally committed gender violence against Ms. Ala. Mr. Pyle's demurrer to the defamation and UCL claims are overruled for the reasons explained in the ruling on the demurrer filed by the CH2M entities. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Ala is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
